source_title: Proximal Biceps Repair Using SwiveLock® Tenodesis System
text: in this animation we will show a biceps tendon repair using a swivel ah canker the biceps attached onto two places in the shoulder the portion of the biceps that inserts onto the socket of the ball and socket joint the long head may be damaged and cause pain in the shoulder moving the biceps from its original location to one lower down the arm relieves this pain this procedure is called a biceps tenodesis first your surgeon places a suture in the biceps tendon and removes it from its original location through small incisions in the skin your surgeon uses a series of instruments to prepare a new hole in the humerus finally the biceps are reattached to the bone by inserting it into the hole and securing it with a swivel anchor the extra tendon is then removed this completes the biceps tendon repair
